Scoring methodology — how RFPs are matched to Quiq Labs

Each RFP is scored 0–10 against Quiq Labs's three divisions:

  • Technology — custom web development (code + WordPress), iOS/Android app development, e-commerce, web applications.
  • Creative Studio — photography, branding, graphic design, illustration, podcasting (audio + video), videography.
  • Education — After-school STEAM enrichment, Pop-Up STEAM, field trips, Resource Portal lesson-planning SaaS (1,400+ lessons, mobile app), Hero Academy CTE.

Score signals: 9–10 = bullseye · 7–8 = strong fit · 5–6 = partial fit, may need a partner · 3–4 = peripheral · 0–2 = outside core.

Portals being monitored statewide
  • PlanetBids: Fresno (14769), Clovis (14742), CSU Fresno (26037), State Center CCD (51469), Stanislaus County (14599), SJJPA (39501), City of Sacramento (15300), City of Salinas (22949), plus dozens more statewide.
  • PublicPurchase: County of Fresno + many CA agencies.
  • BidNet Direct California: 3,000+ statewide solicitations across 764 agencies.
  • RFPMart California listings: aggregated web design, marketing, mobile, software RFPs.
  • ProcureWare, OpenGov, DemandStar, Bonfire, HigherGov, SAM.gov, CalProcure: spot-checked for matches.
  • Agency direct: Visalia, Hanford, Tulare, Bakersfield CSD, Fresno EOC, Fresno Superior Court.
